evals.prompt: Preciso de preparar a agenda para uma reunião amanhã com o André para decidir o pricing do novo serviço. Dura 30 minutos.
evals.expected_output: A meeting agenda using the standard template (25/50-min default, not 30), with the gate check applied (decision meeting passes), pre-reads listed, agenda items with owner/time/type, and the template distributed 24h in advance.
expectations: The skill applies the agenda-or-decline gate: this is a decision meeting (pricing decision) — it passes gate question 2.
The meeting slot is flagged if it falls during the First-Four-Hours block or on a deep-work day without justification.
The agenda template uses a 25-minute slot (not 30 — the 5-minute buffer rule), or explicitly justifies a 50-minute slot if complexity warrants it.
The agenda includes Objective (the single decision needed), at least one Pre-read, numbered agenda items with time/lead/type, and a 5-minute 'Decisions and next steps' slot at the end.
The agenda is flagged for distribution at least 24 hours before the meeting — the skill notes the 24h rule explicitly.

evals.prompt: Got-a-minute? Can we jump on a quick call to chat about the marketing campaign? It'll be 10 minutes.
evals.expected_output: A got-a-minute containment response deferring the request to the next Mon/Fri got-a-minute slot, with an async alternative offered.
expectations: The skill identifies this as a got-a-minute request (unscheduled, no agenda, 'quick call', undefined outcome).
The containment response offers the next available Mon/Fri got-a-minute slot — not a same-day or deep-day slot.
An async alternative is offered: voice note, 2-paragraph written summary, or Loom.
If today is a deep-work day (Tue/Wed/Thu), the response explicitly defers per calendar-defense Rule 4 — no exceptions unless a genuine emergency is stated.
The got-a-minute is logged in the follow-up tracker as a deferred topic.

evals.prompt: Write the minutes for the meeting we just had: we decided to use HubSpot Free as our CRM, André will set it up by June 20th, and we parked the question of paid upgrades for next month's review.
evals.expected_output: Decision-focused minutes using the standard template, with decisions table (decision/owner/deadline), actions table (action/owner/deadline), and a parking-lot entry with routing.
expectations: The minutes are written in the decision record format — not as a transcript or discussion summary.
The HubSpot decision is recorded as a declarative sentence ('We will use HubSpot Free as the CRM') with owner (André) and deadline (2026-06-20).
The parking-lot item (paid upgrades question) has a routing destination (named next meeting date or named person) — not left as an open item with no route.
The minutes are flagged for distribution within 1 hour of the meeting ending (or end of business day at latest).
Significant meeting decisions are queued for kb_ingest with the decisions and actions tables — not the discussion.
